title: fix: workflow API error standardization
body:
## Summary

Closes #7487 — Error Standardization: Workflow API.

### Changes

**`pkg/api/universal/workflow.go`**
- Add `validateWorkflowComponent` helper and call it at the top of all 7 workflow handlers (Get, Start, Terminate, RaiseEvent, Pause, Resume, Purge). When `WorkflowComponent` is empty the handlers now return `ErrNoOrMissingWorkflowComponent` (`ERR_WORKFLOW_COMPONENT_MISSING`, HTTP 400). These two error codes (`ERR_WORKFLOW_COMPONENT_MISSING` / `ERR_WORKFLOW_COMPONENT_NOT_FOUND`) were defined in `messages/predefined.go` and `errorcodes/errorcodes.go` but never exercised by any handler.
- Fix raw `return nil, err` in `StartWorkflow` for `uuid.NewRandom()` failure: now wraps with `messages.ErrStartWorkflow` and logs consistently.
- Fix `GetWorkflow`: when `api.ErrInstanceNotFound`, return early rather than setting `err = nil` and calling `a.logger.Debug(nil)`.

**`pkg/api/universal/workflow_test.go`**
- Add `TestPurgeWorkflowApi` (was missing entirely).
- Add a "No workflow component provided" test case to all 7 test functions asserting `ErrNoOrMissingWorkflowComponent`.

**`pkg/api/http/http_test.go`**
- Add HTTP-layer error tests:
  - Get workflow instance not found → 404 `ERR_INSTANCE_ID_NOT_FOUND`
  - Terminate with engine error → 500 `ERR_TERMINATE_WORKFLOW`
  - Pause with engine error → 500 `ERR_PAUSE_WORKFLOW`
  - Resume with engine error → 500 `ERR_RESUME_WORKFLOW`

comments.body: This only rejects an empty workflow component. Because the handlers do not otherwise use the component name and `ErrWorkflowComponentDoesNotExist` remains unused, a request with any non-empty but nonexistent component still proceeds against the default workflow engine instead of returning `ERR_WORKFLOW_COMPONENT_NOT_FOUND` (see `pkg/messages/predefined.go:129`). If the API is meant to validate the workflow component path/request field, this helper needs to distinguish missing from not-found components.

Fixed in commit 4f8a3c37 — added a `workflowEngine == nil` guard so a non-empty component name with no configured engine now returns `ErrWorkflowComponentDoesNotExist` rather than proceeding.

For full component-name-to-engine validation (the case where an engine is configured but the supplied name doesn’t match): Dapr’s `Universal` holds a single `workflowEngine` field rather than a name-keyed registry, so wiring that up would require storing the registered component name at engine setup time — happy to add that in a follow-up if the team considers it in scope for this PR.

files.patch:
@@ -2686,6 +2686,95 @@ func TestV1Workflow(t *testing.T) {
 		// assert
 		assert.Nil(t, resp.ErrorBody)
 	})
+
+	////////////////////////////
+	// GET NOT-FOUND TESTS   //
+	////////////////////////////
+
+	t.Run("Get workflow instance not found", func(t *testing.T) {
+		// When the workflow engine returns a response with nil Properties,
+		// the HTTP handler should return 404 with ERR_INSTANCE_ID_NOT_FOUND.
+		wf.WithClient(func() workflows.Workflow {
+			return fake.NewClient().WithGet(func(ctx context.Context, req *workflows.GetRequest) (*workflows.StateResponse, error) {
+				return &workflows.StateResponse{
+					Workflow: &workflows.WorkflowState{
+						InstanceID: req.InstanceID,
+						Properties: nil,
+					},
+				}, nil
+			})
+		})
+
+		apiPath := "v1.0/workflows/dapr/notExistingInstanceID"
+		resp := fakeServer(t).DoRequest("GET", apiPath, nil, nil)
+		assert.Equal(t, 404, resp.StatusCode)
+
+		// assert
+		assert.NotNil(t, resp.ErrorBody)
+		assert.Equal(t, "ERR_INSTANCE_ID_NOT_FOUND", resp.ErrorBody["errorCode"])
+		assert.Equal(t, messages.ErrWorkflowInstanceNotFound.WithFormat("notExistingInstanceID").Message(), resp.ErrorBody["message"])
+	})
+
+	///////////////////////////////
+	// TERMINATE ERROR TESTS    //
+	///////////////////////////////
+
+	t.Run("Terminate with engine error returns standardized error code", func(t *testing.T) {
+		wf.WithClient(func() workflows.Workflow {
+			return fake.NewClient().WithTerminate(func(ctx context.Context, req *workflows.TerminateRequest) error {
+				return errors.New("engine failure")
+			})
+		})
+
+		apiPath := "v1.0/workflows/dapr/instanceID/terminate"
+		resp := fakeServer(t).DoRequest("POST", apiPath, nil, nil)
+		assert.Equal(t, 500, resp.StatusCode)
+
+		// assert
+		assert.NotNil(t, resp.ErrorBody)
+		assert.Equal(t, "ERR_TERMINATE_WORKFLOW", resp.ErrorBody["errorCode"])
+	})
+
+	///////////////////////////
+	// PAUSE ERROR TESTS    //
+	///////////////////////////
+
+	t.Run("Pause with engine error returns standardized error code", func(t *testing.T) {
+		wf.WithClient(func() workflows.Workflow {
+			return fake.NewClient().WithPause(func(ctx context.Context, req *workflows.PauseRequest) error {
+				return errors.New("engine failure")
+			})
+		})
+
+		apiPath := "v1.0/workflows/dapr/instanceID/pause"
+		resp := fakeServer(t).DoRequest("POST", apiPath, nil, nil)
+		assert.Equal(t, 500, resp.StatusCode)
+
+		// assert
+		assert.NotNil(t, resp.ErrorBody)
+		assert.Equal(t, "ERR_PAUSE_WORKFLOW", resp.ErrorBody["errorCode"])
+	})
+
+	////////////////////////////
+	// RESUME ERROR TESTS    //
+	////////////////////////////
+
+	t.Run("Resume with engine error returns standardized error code", func(t *testing.T) {
+		wf.WithClient(func() workflows.Workflow {
+			return fake.NewClient().WithResume(func(ctx context.Context, req *workflows.ResumeRequest) error {
+				return errors.New("engine failure")
+			})
+		})
+
+		apiPath := "v1.0/workflows/dapr/instanceID/resume"
+		resp := fakeServer(t).DoRequest("POST", apiPath, nil, nil)
+		assert.Equal(t, 500, resp.StatusCode)
+
+		// assert
+		assert.NotNil(t, resp.ErrorBody)
+		assert.Equal(t, "ERR_RESUME_WORKFLOW", resp.ErrorBody["errorCode"])
+	})
+
 }
 
 func buildHTTPPipeline(spec config.PipelineSpec) middleware.HTTP {
